tasks.title: Epic — wtfm support for Taproot's Help & Docs Surfaces initiative
tasks.summary: Umbrella for the wtfm capabilities Taproot's Help & Docs Surfaces initiative needs — stable anchors, surface pages, an ESP0119-conformant help-document mode, a help manifest with drift checking, and path-prefix portability — coordinating sequencing and the cross-repo contracts.

tasks.title: Emit stable heading anchors across rendered docs
tasks.summary: Give every rendered heading a deterministic, documented id — section headings, per-item headings, and markdown guide content — with a JSDoc override tag so anchors can be pinned to Espalier field-names, making wtfm output URL-addressable at section granularity.

tasks.title: Surface pages — one doc page per unit of display
tasks.summary: Let components declare membership in a named surface (a unit of display like a page editor or form) and generate one composed doc page per surface — declaration tags, a surfaces data helper for nav and URLs, and rendering that composes member components in order.

tasks.title: Help-document output mode conforming to ESP0119 strip-to-bare
tasks.summary: A lean render mode that emits one help document per surface whose markup survives Espalier ESP0119's strip-to-bare normalization — semantic elements and stable field-name anchors only, plain code instead of interactive demos — so fetched help renders cleanly inside the flyout.

tasks.title: Help manifest and anchor drift check
tasks.summary: Emit a machine-readable help manifest (surface → URLs → anchor list) at build time and ship a check command that fails when a consumer-supplied expected-anchor list no longer matches the built docs — turning silent anchor drift into a CI failure.

tasks.title: Path-prefix portability for generated sites
tasks.summary: Make wtfm-generated URLs, breadcrumbs, nav data, and client-asset references compose correctly with an Eleventy pathPrefix so a docs site can serve from a sub-path like /help/ on an app origin — the serving contract Taproot's help site depends on.

tasks.title: Publish @taprootio/wtfm to the public npm registry
tasks.summary: Move publishing from the private GitHub Packages registry to the public npm registry so @taprootio/wtfm installs without GitHub auth; ship 0.13.0 as the first public release.

tasks.title: Switch npm publishing to Trusted Publishing (OIDC)
tasks.summary: Configure npm Trusted Publishing so the publish workflow authenticates via GitHub Actions OIDC, then retire the short-lived NPM_TOKEN secret entirely.
